name: Randy Dobnak
reasoning: Bleacher Nation and MLB.com both confirm Dobnak struck out only three batters in 4 1/3 innings in his last outing, and a dedicated props article cites an 11.3% strikeout rate and low-whiff sinker as reasons to bet under 3.5 strikeouts at -140. THE BAT projections rank Dobnak in the 4th percentile of all starters and note he's on a below-average pitch leash, both of which point to limited innings/opportunities to rack up strikeouts.
article_consensus: Multiple previews frame this as a mismatch: red-hot rookie Troy Melton (5-1, 1.80 ERA) at home for Detroit against Randy Dobnak, a back-end/short-leash arm with a modest strikeout profile (4.5 K/9, ranked in the 4th percentile among SPs by THE BAT) coming off a 4.1-inning, 3-strikeout outing against San Diego; the shared angle points toward fewer Dobnak strikeouts and a Tigers-favored game script.
